one_time_description: Involuntary disposition: loss on disposal of tangible capital assets of $558,823 partially offset by gain on deferred contributions related to capital assets of $408,377
economic_notes: Nation holds 29% interest in Projet Nadagam; holds shares in Coopérative de Solidarité Wenicec; has forestry operations (CSEF sector); economic development activities include café/restaurant, dépanneur, mechanic workshop, tourism project, artisan project, catering service, and cooperative enterprises.
notes: Audit is qualified (opinion avec réserve) due to non-presentation of originally budgeted amounts as required by SP 1201. The financial statements use fund accounting with four funds: administration, reserve, capital assets, and trust. Total gross revenues before deferred revenue adjustments are $31,406,505; net revenues recognized are $23,195,894. Total government transfers calculated as sum of all federal and provincial transfer lines. OSR calculated as gross revenue minus identified government transfers. Involuntary disposition resulted in net loss of $150,446. Nation has significant deferred revenue balance of $26.2M (up from $18.5M). Remuneration schedule for chief and council shows total of $218,680 but individual breakdown not provided in extracted text. CSEF (Centre Savoir Être Savoir Faire) is a separate sector within the band. Expenses_salaries_total includes salaires et charges sociales $11,298,401 + rémunération chef et conseillers $218,680 + salaires CSEF $289,682 = $11,806,763. Sectoral expense totals sourced from Note 19 information sectorielle. Long-term debt is fully classified as current (due within one year).
warnings: One-time items ($1) represent 0.0% of revenue — surplus may not reflect ongoing operations
Qualified audit opinion issued — financial statements may not fully reflect true position
